lessonTitle: He Will Swallow Up Death in Victory
quickInsight: Every Old Testament believer looked forward to the same Savior we look back on.

quotableVerses.text: He will swallow up death in victory; and the Lord God will wipe away tears from off all faces.
For as in Adam all die, even so in Christ shall all be made alive.

And he shall go forth, suffering pains and afflictions and temptations of every kind; and this that the word might be fulfilled which saith he will take upon him the pains and the sicknesses of his people. And he will take upon him death, that he may loose the bands of death which bind his people; and he will take upon him their infirmities, that his bowels may be filled with mercy, according to the flesh, that he may know according to the flesh how to succor his people according to their infirmities.
But he was wounded for our transgressions, he was bruised for our iniquities: the chastisement of our peace was upon him; and with his stripes we are healed.

sections.title: Prophecies Fulfilled in Christ
items: Zechariah 9:9 foretold Christ's triumphal entry on a donkey — fulfilled in Matthew 21:1–11.
Zechariah 11:12–13 predicted betrayal for thirty pieces of silver — fulfilled in Matthew 26:14–16.
Psalm 22:16, 18 described pierced hands and feet, and lots cast for clothing — fulfilled at Calvary (John 19:17–18; Matthew 27:35).
Isaiah 25:8 promised death swallowed up in victory — fulfilled on Easter morning (Mark 16:1–6).
Point out: the Old Testament never names 'Jesus Christ,' yet His mission is on every page.

sections.title: What Christ Overcomes for Us
items: The manual groups His power into four categories: sin, death, trials, and weaknesses.
Death — He broke the bands of death so every person born will be resurrected (Alma 11:42–45).
Sin — Through repentance and His grace we can be made clean (Moroni 10:32–33; D&C 19:15–19).
Trials — He descended below all things, so He can lift us through every hard moment (Alma 7:11–13).
Weaknesses — He gives us grace to turn weaknesses into strengths (Ether 12:27; Matthew 11:28–30).

sections.title: Discussion Questions
items: How would you explain to a friend who isn't a member why Jesus Christ matters to you personally?
Isaiah 53:3–5 and D&C 19:16–19 describe the price the Savior paid. What price did Heavenly Father pay? (John 3:16)
When have you felt the Savior's power helping you through something hard — not just forgiving sin, but carrying a burden?
General conference is this Easter weekend. What testimony of the Resurrection stood out to you?

sections.title: Book of Mormon Witnesses of Christ
items: The manual highlights that Book of Mormon prophecies of Christ are even more abundant and clear than the Old Testament.
1 Nephi 11:31–33 — Nephi saw in vision the Savior's ministry, crucifixion, and the multitudes He healed.
2 Nephi 25:13 — Nephi testified plainly that the Messiah would rise from the dead.
Mosiah 3:7 — King Benjamin described the Savior bleeding from every pore — details the Old Testament doesn't give us.
Ask: Why did Heavenly Father give the Restoration, including the Book of Mormon, to make these prophecies clearer?

sections.title: Teaching Ideas
items: Sorting activity: Write the scripture references from the manual on slips of paper. Have the quorum sort them into sin, death, trials, and weaknesses — some fit multiple categories. Discuss why.
Prophecy matching: Put Old Testament prophecies on one side of the board, New Testament fulfillments on the other. Have the young men draw lines connecting them.
Since general conference is this weekend, invite each young man to listen for one testimony of the Resurrection and come ready to share it next week.

sections.title: Spread the Savior's Peace
items: The manual asks: even at Easter, many people don't feel joyful. What can you do to spread the Savior's peace?
Challenge: pick one scripture about Christ's peace (Psalms 30:2–5; Isaiah 40:28–31; John 14:27) and text it to someone who needs it this week.
Consider creating a simple Easter message to share on social media or as a card — the manual specifically suggests this.
Ask: Who in the ward or neighborhood might need the quorum to show up for them this Easter?
